title: 30o: malformed server sha256 maps to Parse not Mismatch
description: validate_sha256_format() checks that the server-returned sha256 field is exactly 64 lowercase hex characters before doing any comparison, and maps a format violation to ClientError::Parse rather than BlobIntegrityMismatch. This is correct because a malformed digest string (wrong length, uppercase, non-hex) means the server sent a structurally invalid response, which is a parse/protocol error. Treating it as an integrity mismatch would be misleading: callers would log 'expected <garbage>, got <real hash>', confusing the remediation path. The format check also prevents the comparison from silently passing due to a truncated or padded value.
close_reason: decision record: malformed sha256 as Parse is correct
